question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: RecordFile (wrappedRecordBlockHash field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a block stream record that produces a `RecordFile` with a maximally-sized `byte[] wrappedRecordBlockHash`, and when combined with `previousWrappedRecordBlockHash` and `bytes`, cause per-instance memory to exceed available heap when the importer processes a burst of such files concurrently?
